data.description: Universal SEO skill for Claude Code. 25 sub-skills + 18 sub-agents covering technical SEO, E-E-A-T, schema, GEO/AEO, backlinks, local SEO, maps intelligence, semantic clustering, e-commerce SEO, international SEO, Google APIs, and PDF/Excel reporting. Optional DataForSEO, Firecrawl, and Banana extensions. - AgriciDaniel/claude-seo

Question-based citability scoring, primary-source evidence on llms.txt, IPTC \u003ccode\u003eTrainedAlgorithmicMedia\u003c/code\u003e for AI-generated product images, agent-friendly page checks per \u003ca href=\"https://web.dev/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eweb.dev\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eParallel execution.\u003c/strong\u003e Full site audits spawn up to 15 specialist agents simultaneously. Site-level audits complete in minutes rather than hours.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFalsifiable, not promotional.\u003c/strong\u003e Every recommendation carries the first-principle observation it rests on, its dependency relationships, an explicit \"how would we know this failed?\" check, and a leading indicator. dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat Core Web Vitals does Claude SEO check?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-core-web-vitals-does-claude-seo-check\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What Core Web Vitals does Claude SEO check?\" href=\"#what-core-web-vitals-does-claude-seo-check\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eClaude SEO measures the current three Core Web Vitals: \u003cstrong\u003eLCP\u003c/strong\u003e (Largest Contentful Paint, target under 2.5s), \u003cstrong\u003eINP\u003c/strong\u003e (Interaction to Next Paint, target under 200ms), and \u003cstrong\u003eCLS\u003c/strong\u003e (Cumulative Layout Shift, target under 0.1). \u003ca href=\"https://web.dev/articles/inp\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eINP replaced FID\u003c/a\u003e on March 12, 2024; FID was removed from all Chrome tools (CrUX API, PageSpeed Insights, Lighthouse) on September 9, 2024, and Claude SEO never references FID. Field data comes from the Chrome User Experience Report (CrUX) when available; lab data falls back to Lighthouse via PageSpeed Insights. LCP can be decomposed into subparts (TTFB, load delay, load duration, render delay) via the \u003ccode\u003e/seo google\u003c/code\u003e CrUX integration to localize bottlenecks. Mobile and desktop are measured separately. CrUX History (25-week trend) is included in the Tier 0 free credential set.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow does Claude SEO assess E-E-A-T?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-does-claude-seo-assess-e-e-a-t\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How does Claude SEO assess E-E-A-T?\" href=\"#how-does-claude-seo-assess-e-e-a-t\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eE-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) is evaluated against the Search Quality Rater Guidelines, last updated September 2025 with YMYL expanded to include political and social topics. Experience signals: original research, case studies, first-hand photos. Expertise: author credentials and topical depth. Authoritativeness: external citations and brand mentions. Trustworthiness, the most heavily weighted of the four: contact info, secure HTTPS, transparent corrections, date stamps. Before scoring sub-factors, Claude SEO applies Google's own Who / How / Why heuristic from the \u003ca href=\"https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/creating-helpful-content\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003ehelpful-content guide\u003c/a\u003e. Generative AI content is fine if it meets Search Essentials; it crosses into spam when used to scale low-value pages, which \u003ccode\u003eseo-content humanize\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eseo-content verify\u003c/code\u003e are designed to detect.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat Schema.org types does Claude SEO support?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-schemaorg-types-does-claude-seo-support\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What Schema.org types does Claude SEO support?\" href=\"#what-schemaorg-types-does-claude-seo-support\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eJSON-LD is the preferred format (Google's stated preference). Active types Claude SEO detects, validates, and generates: Organization, LocalBusiness, Article, BlogPosting, NewsArticle, Product, ProductGroup, Offer, Review, AggregateRating, BreadcrumbList, WebSite, WebPage, Person, ProfilePage, ContactPage, VideoObject, ImageObject, Event, JobPosting, Course, DiscussionForumPosting, Reservation, OrderAction, plus video and specialized types (BroadcastEvent, Clip, SeekToAction, SoftwareSourceCode). Restricted: FAQ (government and healthcare authority sites only since August 2023). Deprecated and never recommended: HowTo (rich results removed September 2023), SpecialAnnouncement (July 2025), ClaimReview, VehicleListing, EstimatedSalary, LearningVideo, CourseInfo carousel (all retired June 2025). Replacement guidance: \u003ca href=\"/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/skills/seo-schema/references/deprecated-types-2024-2026.md\"\u003eskills/seo-schema/references/deprecated-types-2024-2026.md\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow does Claude SEO optimize for AI search?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-does-claude-seo-optimize-for-ai-search\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How does Claude SEO optimize for AI search?\" href=\"#how-does-claude-seo-optimize-for-ai-search\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAligned with \u003ca href=\"https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eGoogle's AI Optimization Guide\u003c/a\u003e, which states that \"AEO\" and \"GEO\" are rebranded labels for SEO. AI Overviews and AI Mode are grounded in the same ranking systems as classic Search; pages must be indexed and eligible for snippet display to appear in any AI feature. Claude SEO scores passage citability (optimal 134-167 word self-contained answer blocks), question-based heading hierarchy, attribution density, structured data coverage, and entity presence across Wikipedia, Reddit, YouTube, and LinkedIn. The \u003ccode\u003eseo-geo\u003c/code\u003e skill includes evidence-based reframes of three popular myths: llms.txt is not currently a citation lever (\u003ca href=\"/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/skills/seo-geo/references/llmstxt-evidence.md\"\u003eprimary-source evidence\u003c/a\u003e), content chunking is not required, and AI-specific keyword rewriting is unnecessary because synonym understanding is sufficient.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhich Google SEO APIs does Claude SEO integrate with?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-which-google-seo-apis-does-claude-seo-integrate-with\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Which Google SEO APIs does Claude SEO integrate with?\" href=\"#which-google-seo-apis-does-claude-seo-integrate-with\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eA 4-tier credential system lets you start with zero keys and add data as needed. Every tier delivers real value at its level:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eTier\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eCredentials\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eAPIs Unlocked\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e0\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAPI key\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePageSpeed Insights, CrUX, CrUX History (25-week trends)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e1\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e+ OAuth or Service Account\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e+ Search Console (queries, URL Inspection, sitemap status), Indexing API\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e2\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e+ GA4 property config\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e+ GA4 organic traffic, top landing pages, device / country breakdown\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e3\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e+ Ads developer token\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e+ Keyword Planner search volume and competition data\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePDF reports are generated via \u003ca href=\"https://weasyprint.org/\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eWeasyPrint\u003c/a\u003e (A4 layout) with matplotlib charts at 200 DPI. Run \u003ccode\u003e/seo google setup\u003c/code\u003e for the credential wizard. All credentials live under \u003ccode\u003e~/.config/claude-seo/\u003c/code\u003e with \u003ccode\u003e0o600\u003c/code\u003e permissions; nothing is checked into the repo.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow does Claude SEO handle local SEO?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-does-claude-seo-handle-local-seo\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How does Claude SEO handle local SEO?\" href=\"#how-does-claude-seo-handle-local-seo\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThree layers. \u003cstrong\u003eGoogle Business Profile signals\u003c/strong\u003e: categories, hours, photos, posts, products, attributes. \u003cstrong\u003eNAP consistency\u003c/strong\u003e across citations: name, address, phone matched against major directories with deviation flagging. \u003cstrong\u003eReview intelligence\u003c/strong\u003e: rating trends, sentiment, response coverage. For multi-location businesses, Claude SEO enforces a 30-page warning threshold and a 50-page hard stop to prevent doorway-page violations (configurable). The \u003ccode\u003e/seo maps\u003c/code\u003e workflow adds geo-grid rank tracking, GBP profile auditing, and competitor radius mapping. Local schema generation covers \u003ccode\u003eLocalBusiness\u003c/code\u003e with all required and recommended properties (geo coordinates, opening hours, areaServed). Phase F (v2) added a GBP deprecation linter that detects retired chat-field references and \u003ccode\u003e.business.site\u003c/code\u003e URLs.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe plugin follows the \u003ca href=\"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/skills\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eAgent Skills standard\u003c/a\u003e with a 3-layer architecture (directive, orchestration, execution). Skills and agents are auto-discovered from \u003ccode\u003eskills/seo-*/\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eagents/seo-*.md\u003c/code\u003e. The orchestrator (\u003ccode\u003eskills/seo/SKILL.md\u003c/code\u003e) handles industry detection (SaaS, local, ecommerce, publisher, agency), parallel sub-agent dispatch up to 15 simultaneously, and synthesis through the \u003ca href=\"#methodology\"\u003e10-principle framework\u003c/a\u003e before emitting the action plan. Full architecture: \u003ca href=\"/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\"\u003edocs/ARCHITECTURE.md\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" thinking framework: radial wheel with PERCEIVE, ANALYZE, VALIDATE, ACT phases and 10 principles arranged by quadrant\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEvery audit walks 10 principles grouped into four phases. Each emitted recommendation carries four fields: the first-principle observation it rests on, its dependency relationship to other recommendations, a \"how would we know this failed?\" check, and a leading indicator to monitor.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003ePhase\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003ePrinciples\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eWhat it does\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePERCEIVE\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eOBSERVE (external) · OBSERVE (internal) · LISTEN\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCollect raw signals; audit your own assumptions; read what the SERP, the brand voice, and the community actually say\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eANALYZE\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTHINK · CONNECT (lateral) · CONNECT (system)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eReduce to first principles; find non-obvious cross-skill links; sequence into a dependency graph\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVALIDATE\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eFEEL · ACCEPT\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePressure-test against UX, brand voice, operator capacity; surface falsifiability\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eACT\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCREATE · GROW\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eShip the artifact; set the feedback loop for the next audit\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFull methodology: \u003ca href=\"/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/skills/seo/references/thinking-framework.md\"\u003eskills/seo/references/thinking-framework.md\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat's New in v2\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-whats-new-in-v2\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What's New in v2\" href=\"#whats-new-in-v2\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ev2.0.0 is the largest release in the plugin's history. Six build phases, all shipped:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase A: Headless rendering everywhere.\u003c/strong\u003e Shared \u003ccode\u003escripts/render_page.py\u003c/code\u003e with Playwright Chromium plus \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/adbar/trafilatura\"\u003etrafilatura\u003c/a\u003e and \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/adbar/htmldate\"\u003ehtmldate\u003c/a\u003e. Every audit subagent gets SPA-aware fetching via \u003ccode\u003e--render auto\u003c/code\u003e (auto-detected on Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Astro islands). Closes the SPA limitation that capped v1.x.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase B: QRG-aligned content quality gates.\u003c/strong\u003e Filler detector and AI-pattern humanizer keyed to QRG §4.6.5 and §4.6.6, claim-verification scanner, expired-domain heritage check via WHOIS, primary-source Google updates changelog.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase C: Technical and CWV depth.\u003c/strong\u003e LCP subparts via CrUX (TTFB, load delay, load duration, render delay), Speculation Rules and bfcache detection, IndexNow submitter for Bing / Yandex / Seznam / Naver, Unlighthouse multi-page Lighthouse wrapper.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase D: Schema completeness.\u003c/strong\u003e Four explicit generators (Reservation, OrderAction, DiscussionForumPosting, ProfilePage), e-commerce schema validator (\u003ccode\u003ehasMerchantReturnPolicy\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eshippingDetails\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eMemberProgram\u003c/code\u003e, EU \u003ccode\u003eenergyEfficiencyClass\u003c/code\u003e, ProductGroup variants), dual validator (Rich Results Test plus Schema Markup Validator).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase E: AI search reframing and 5 new MCP extensions.\u003c/strong\u003e Ahrefs, SE Ranking (AI Share-of-Voice), Profound (LLM citation tracker), Bing Webmaster plus IndexNow, Unlighthouse. Plus the parasite-SEO risk scanner per Google's November 2024 \u003ca href=\"https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2024/11/site-reputation-abuse-update\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003esite reputation abuse policy\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePhase F: Local, international, and privacy polish.\u003c/strong\u003e Google Business Profile deprecation linter (chat field, \u003ccode\u003e.business.site\u003c/code\u003e URLs, Q\u0026amp;A), DMA consent-mode-v2 click-through diagnostic, machine-translation QA flag per January 2025 QRG.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTest coverage: 248 → 271 (a 5.4× increase over the v1.9.9 baseline). 83 SSRF and DNS-rebinding bypass tests close the full obfuscated-IPv4, FQDN-trailing-dot, and redirect-rebinding bypass classes. Full migration notes and breaking changes: \u003ca href=\"/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/docs/MIGRATION-v1-to-v2.md\"\u003edocs/MIGRATION-v1-to-v2.md\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHeavy client-side hydration timing.\u003c/strong\u003e Phase A's headless renderer handles most SPAs out of the box (\u003ccode\u003e--render auto\u003c/code\u003e detects empty \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;div id=\"root\"\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e shells and switches to Playwright). Edge cases that still produce noisy findings: pages with hydration tied to scroll position past the fold, pages that fetch critical content after user interaction (modal opens, tab clicks), pages with race-condition-prone third-party widget mounts. For these, manually triggering the \u003ccode\u003eseo-visual\u003c/code\u003e subagent and comparing its Playwright snapshot to the raw-HTML subagents' findings is the recommended workflow.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eLocal-only without enrichment.\u003c/strong\u003e The free tier does not call any external API and produces zero-network output. Adding Google API credentials (Tier 0 through 3) unlocks real field data and live indexation status; without them, Core Web Vitals are lab estimates only and indexation is inferred from page-level signals. Adding MCP extensions (Ahrefs, DataForSEO, SE Ranking, Profound) similarly unlocks competitive and AI-citation data but requires their respective accounts.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat is Claude SEO?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-is-claude-seo\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What is Claude SEO?\" href=\"#what-is-claude-seo\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eClaude SEO is an open-source SEO analysis plugin for Claude Code. It runs 25 sub-skills and 18 specialist agents in parallel across technical SEO, content quality, Schema.org markup, AI search optimization, local SEO, e-commerce, and international SEO. Audits produce a prioritized action plan where each recommendation carries the first-principle observation it rests on, its dependency relationship to other recommendations, a \"how would we know this failed?\" check, and a leading indicator. The plugin is MIT-licensed, ships zero proprietary tracking, and works fully offline if you skip the optional Google API and MCP-extension enrichments. Aligned with \u003ca href=\"https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eGoogle's AI Optimization Guide\u003c/a\u003e and the September 2025 Quality Rater Guidelines.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow is Claude SEO different from Screaming Frog or Ahrefs Site Audit?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-is-claude-seo-different-from-screaming-frog-or-ahrefs-site-audit\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How is Claude SEO different from Screaming Frog or Ahrefs Site Audit?\" href=\"#how-is-claude-seo-different-from-screaming-frog-or-ahrefs-site-audit\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDifferent surface area, different tradeoffs. \u003cstrong\u003eScreaming Frog\u003c/strong\u003e crawls deeper and faster at the link-graph level; it is purpose-built as a crawler and Claude SEO does not attempt to replace it. \u003cstrong\u003eAhrefs Site Audit\u003c/strong\u003e brings a proprietary backlink index and link intelligence; Claude SEO integrates with Ahrefs via its MCP extension rather than competing. Where Claude SEO leads: conversational LLM-native workflow, recommendation falsifiability (every finding carries an explicit failure-mode check), open-source MIT licensing with zero per-domain pricing, AI search optimization aligned with Google's primary-source guidance, and primary-source schema-deprecation tracking. Use Screaming Frog or Ahrefs for what they are best at; use Claude SEO when you want LLM-driven synthesis, conversational iteration, and AI-search-first audits in the same environment as your other Claude Code workflows.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eDoes Claude SEO work on single-page applications (Next.js, React, Vue)?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-does-claude-seo-work-on-single-page-applications-nextjs-react-vue\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Does Claude SEO work on single-page applications (Next.js, React, Vue)?\" href=\"#does-claude-seo-work-on-single-page-applications-nextjs-react-vue\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes. Phase A of v2 shipped a shared headless renderer (\u003ccode\u003escripts/render_page.py\u003c/code\u003e) backed by Playwright Chromium. Every fetching subagent supports \u003ccode\u003e--render auto\u003c/code\u003e (the default), which auto-detects SPA hallmarks (empty \u003ccode\u003e\u0026lt;div id=\"root\"\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e shells, single bundle script, hydration markers) and switches to a rendered fetch. Set \u003ccode\u003e--render always\u003c/code\u003e to force rendering, or \u003ccode\u003e--render never\u003c/code\u003e for raw HTML only. Content extraction uses \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/adbar/trafilatura\"\u003etrafilatura\u003c/a\u003e for boilerplate removal. Publication dates come from \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/adbar/htmldate\"\u003ehtmldate\u003c/a\u003e. Known nuance: pages with scroll-bound hydration or post-interaction content fetches still produce noisy findings; see the \u003ca href=\"#limitations\"\u003eLimitations\u003c/a\u003e section for the recommended \u003ccode\u003eseo-visual\u003c/code\u003e cross-check workflow on those edge cases.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhat Google APIs does Claude SEO use, and are they required?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-what-google-apis-does-claude-seo-use-and-are-they-required\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What Google APIs does Claude SEO use, and are they required?\" href=\"#what-google-apis-does-claude-seo-use-and-are-they-required\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNone are required. Claude SEO is fully functional with zero API keys. A 4-tier credential system lets you upgrade gradually: Tier 0 (API key only) unlocks PageSpeed Insights, CrUX, and CrUX History (25-week trend data). Tier 1 (+ OAuth or service account) adds Search Console with queries, URL Inspection, sitemap status, and the Indexing API. Tier 2 (+ GA4 property config) adds organic traffic, top landing pages, and device / country breakdowns. Tier 3 (+ Ads developer token) adds Keyword Planner search volume and competition data. The credential setup wizard runs via \u003ccode\u003e/seo google setup\u003c/code\u003e. All credentials live under \u003ccode\u003e~/.config/claude-seo/\u003c/code\u003e with \u003ccode\u003e0o600\u003c/code\u003e file permissions; nothing is checked into the repo and nothing is transmitted beyond Google's own endpoints.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eIs Claude SEO free?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-is-claude-seo-free\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Is Claude SEO free?\" href=\"#is-claude-seo-free\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYes. MIT licensed, fully open source, no per-domain pricing, no telemetry, no API quotas imposed by the plugin itself. The core plugin and all 25 sub-skills work without any paid service. Some optional MCP extensions wrap paid services (DataForSEO, Ahrefs, Profound, SE Ranking) where you bring your own account credentials; their use is opt-in and the plugin works fully without them. Google APIs (PageSpeed Insights, Search Console, Indexing, GA4) are free from Google with normal account quota limits and require your own credentials. If you want commercial support or enterprise features beyond the open-source plugin, that is not part of this project.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow is Claude SEO different from regular SEO tools when it comes to AI search?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-is-claude-seo-different-from-regular-seo-tools-when-it-comes-to-ai-search\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How is Claude SEO different from regular SEO tools when it comes to AI search?\" href=\"#how-is-claude-seo-different-from-regular-seo-tools-when-it-comes-to-ai-search\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMost SEO tools treat AI search as a separate optimization discipline. Claude SEO follows \u003ca href=\"https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eGoogle's own position\u003c/a\u003e that AEO and GEO are rebranded labels for SEO. AI Overviews and AI Mode are grounded in the same ranking systems as classic Search; the eligibility floor is normal indexation. Claude SEO scores passage citability (134-167 word self-contained answer blocks), question-based heading hierarchy, attribution density, and entity presence across Wikipedia, Reddit, YouTube, and LinkedIn. It explicitly rejects three influencer myths: llms.txt as a citation lever, content chunking for AI, and AI-specific keyword rewriting. For commerce sites, Claude SEO audits the IPTC \u003ccode\u003eTrainedAlgorithmicMedia\u003c/code\u003e requirement on AI-generated product images per Google Merchant Center policy.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eCommunity Contributors\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(ci): install requirements.txt so tests can import requests-using scripts

Root cause: the test job ran `pip install pytest beautifulsoup4` which
left out `requests`. Phase J/K (commit 6778786) added test files that
import scripts through `scripts/url_safety.py`, which raises a RuntimeError
on missing `requests` at module-import time. All 6 collection errors trace
back to this single missing dependency:

  scripts/url_safety.py:82: in <module>
      raise RuntimeError(
  E   RuntimeError: scripts/url_safety.py requires the 'requests' package.

Affected tests (all failed at collection):
- tests/test_gbp_lint_and_polish.py
- tests/test_parasite_risk_and_extensions.py
- tests/test_phase_j_executable.py
- tests/test_render_page.py
- tests/test_technical_depth.py
- tests/test_url_safety.py

Fix: install from requirements.txt + pytest. This matches the documented
user install path (bash install.sh -> pip install -r requirements.txt)
and keeps CI in sync with the runtime contract. Phase J/K and future test
additions will continue to work as long as their imports stay within
requirements.txt.

Trade-off: CI install step goes from ~10s to ~45-60s. Acceptable given
that the prior config produced false negatives.

Blast radius: CI test job only. No effect on local dev, install.sh, or
runtime behavior.

Undo: revert this commit; CI returns to the prior minimal install.

Verified: 271/271 tests pass locally with the current requirements.txt.
YAML well-formedness checked with python yaml.safe_load." class="Link--secondary" href="/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/commit/d8ac993e67dd4af48a606f37d81cfd9eb86262e4">fix(ci): install requirements.txt so class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat(agents): wire 8 fetcher subagents to scripts/render_page.py

Closes the Phase A subagent-wiring gap. Every agent that fetches HTML
for analysis now points at the shared headless renderer instead of
calling fetch_page or curl directly. The wiring is documentation-only
(an instruction block at the end of each agent file) so the existing
behaviour and frontmatter are preserved.

Each agent gets the same canonical block:

  Use python scripts/render_page.py <URL> --mode auto --json
  - auto: raw fetch, only spins up Playwright on SPA detection
  - always: force Playwright render
  - never: raw HTML only
  Result JSON exposes raw_content, content, is_spa, extracted_text
  (trafilatura), publication_date (htmldate).
  SSRF + DNS-rebinding live in scripts/url_safety.py — never call
  requests.get directly on user-supplied URLs.

Plus a per-agent recommendation:
  technical: default auto (covers SSR + SPA)
  content:   use extracted_text for E-E-A-T scoring (strip boilerplate)
  schema:    prefer always (client-injected JSON-LD on SPA sites)
  geo:       use extracted_text for passage-level citation scoring
  local:     prefer always (map embeds + review carousels load via JS)
  ecommerce: prefer always (client-injected product schema)
  backlinks: prefer never for bulk verify (a-tags reliably in raw HTML)
  sxo:       prefer always (persona scoring needs rendered DOM)

Affected files (8):
  agents/seo-technical.md
  agents/seo-content.md
  agents/seo-schema.md
  agents/seo-geo.md
  agents/seo-local.md
  agents/seo-ecommerce.md
  agents/seo-backlinks.md
  agents/seo-sxo.md

Tests still green: 149 passed.
Local-only commit on v2. No push.

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat: Phase J + K + audit cleanup on top of v2.0.0

Three layers of post-release work on the v2 branch. The v2.0.0 tag
remains pinned at 062c9e4; this is work ready for v2.0.1 or beyond.

Phase J — Google AI Optimization Guide alignment (substance):
- New reference docs sourced from developers.google.com + web.dev:
  google-ai-optimization-guide.md, agent-friendly-pages.md (new
  skills/seo-technical/references/ dir), ucp-universal-commerce-protocol.md
- New executable scripts: ucp_check.py (/.well-known/ucp validator
  with SSRF-safe endpoint probes), iptc_ai_label.py (DigitalSourceType
  TrainedAlgorithmicMedia audit + inject via exiftool wrapper),
  agent_ux_check.py (semantic HTML + accessibility-tree scorer)
- render_page.py extended with extract_accessibility kwarg + --a11y-tree

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(v1.9.8): Phase B bug fixes — Windows hook, OAuth refresh, missing imports, None guards (#81)

* fix(hooks): use 'python' not 'python3' for Windows compatibility

Closes #68. Windows installs only ship 'python' (or 'python.exe') from
the python.org installer or Microsoft Store stub; 'python3' triggers a
Microsoft Store redirect or NotFound, breaking PostToolUse on every
Edit/Write. POSIX systems alias 'python' to Python 3 in modern setups.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(keyword_planner): add missing top-level 'import os'

os.path.expanduser() and os.path.exists() are called at lines 91-92 in
the OAuth token path. Previously, 'import os' only existed inside an
'except ImportError' block (line 44), so when google_auth import
succeeded, os was never imported — NameError at runtime.

Move 'import os' to top-level imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(google_auth): persist oauth_client_path so refresh survives restart

Closes #72. The OAuth flow exchanged the auth code, saved the token, and
printed "Token saved successfully" — but never wrote oauth_client_path to
~/.config/claude-seo/google-api.json. On the next process start, when the
1-hour access token expired, _refresh_oauth_token() looked for
oauth_client_path in config, found nothing, and every Google API call
401'd until the user re-ran --auth.

Changes:
- New _persist_oauth_client_path(creds_path) helper. Reads existing config,
  sets oauth_client_path to the absolute path of client_secret.json,
  atomically writes via tempfile + os.replace. Merges (preserves api_key,
  service_account_path, ga4_property_id, etc.) — never clobbers.
- _exchange_code() takes creds_path and calls the helper after token save.
  Failure to persist warns to stderr but does not fail the auth flow.
- Both call sites (run_oauth_flow + --exchange CLI) pass creds_path through.

Security:
- Stores PATH only. client_secret value itself stays in client_secret.json
  on disk. Verified by unit test (Case 3 below).
- Atomic write avoids partial-write corruption if interrupted mid-flush.

Verification (3 unit cases run during dev):
- Case 1 fresh: oauth_client_path written to empty config
- Case 2 merge: existing api_key/service_account_path preserved
- Case 3 no leak: client_secret never appears in config file

Note: not adding a regression test in tests/ this commit — the OAuth flow
is end-to-end browser-based and the helper itself is the unit-testable
seam, which the dev-time test covers. A formal tests/test_google_auth.py
belongs in Phase E.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(dataforseo_normalize): None-guard the 3 list normalizers

Closes the iteration-on-None failure mode behind #58 (and the
analogous risk for #59 reviews/social paths). normalize_merchant,
normalize_social, and normalize_reviews now early-return [] when items
is None or empty, matching the existing pattern in format_table().

Verification: dev-time check confirms all 3 functions handle None and
[] gracefully (no NoneType iteration errors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(hooks/validate-schema): add 10 MB file-size guard

Bounds memory + hook latency. Real source files almost never exceed
10 MB; bigger inputs are typically minified bundles, generated assets,
or accidental binary writes. The hook now stat()'s the file first and
exits 0 silently for anything over the cap, never reading the bytes.

Closes the DoS surface flagged as HOOK-4 in the audit.

Verification:
- 11 MB synthetic file → exit 0, no read attempted (regression test)
- Small file → unchanged behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

  - ## Architecture: screenshots/architecture-v2.svg (15KB flat) ->

generate_diagrams.py docstring: corrected the "+ 5 locked picks"

Three layers of post-release work on the v2 branch. The v2.0.0 tag
remains pinned at 062c9e4; this is work ready for v2.0.1 or beyond.

Phase J — Google AI Optimization Guide alignment (substance):
- New reference docs sourced from developers.google.com + web.dev:
  google-ai-optimization-guide.md, agent-friendly-pages.md (new
  skills/seo-technical/references/ dir), ucp-universal-commerce-protocol.md
- New executable scripts: ucp_check.py (/.well-known/ucp validator
  with SSRF-safe endpoint probes), iptc_ai_label.py (DigitalSourceType
  TrainedAlgorithmicMedia audit + inject via exiftool wrapper),
  agent_ux_check.py (semantic HTML + accessibility-tree scorer)
- render_page.py extended with extract_accessibility kwarg + --a11y-tree
  CLI flag (Playwright accessibility.snapshot)
- SKILL.md updates: seo-geo (AEO/GEO=SEO reframe per Google's own
  guide), seo-content (Who/How/Why heuristic), seo-images (IPTC for
  AI-generated media + Merchant Center policy), seo-ecommerce (UCP
  section with audit command), seo-technical (agent-friendly pages
  section)
- 22 new tests in tests/test_phase_j_executable.py

Phase K — 10-principle synthesis methodology (process):
- New skills/seo/references/thinking-framework.md documenting the
  PERCEIVE/ANALYZE/VALIDATE/ACT phase grouping (OBSERVE-external,
  OBSERVE-internal, LISTEN, THINK, CONNECT-lateral, CONNECT-system,
  FEEL, ACCEPT, CREATE, GROW) with concrete SEO-audit mappings
- Orchestrator skills/seo/SKILL.md wired: step 13 invokes the
  framework, new Synthesis Methodology section, 4-field
  recommendation contract (first-principle observation, dependency
  relationship, falsifiability check, leading indicator)

Hostile-review fix-and-verify pass (hardening):
- url_safety.py CLI: sys.exit(2) in JSON mode when URL blocked
- extensions/firecrawl install + uninstall scripts made executable
- New test gating executable bits across all extension scripts
- CHANGELOG.md and docs/MIGRATION-v1-to-v2.md updated for v2.0.0

Audit cleanup (hygiene):
- Removed orphan scripts/release_report.py (v1.9.0 historical artifact)
- Removed the matching py_compile line in .github/workflows/ci.yml
- skills/seo-geo/SKILL.md now directly links references/llmstxt-evidence.md
- 14th manifest assertion added: reference-orphan detection in
  tests/test_manifest_consistency.py (catches references/ files
  on disk but not cited anywhere repo-wide)

Test totals: 271 passing (was 248 at the v2.0.0 release commit).
Manifest assertions: 14 of 14 (was 13).

class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat(v2): Checkpoint 7 — multi-platform portability + reframings (Phase G + I)

Closes gap items #13 (multi-platform portability across Codex/Cursor/
Cline/Aider) and the §10 deprecations/reframings from the v2 plan.

AGENTS.md (extended)
- Added Codex CLI, Cline, and Aider to the supported-harnesses list
  alongside the existing Cursor, Cursor Cloud Agents, Antigravity, and
  Gemini CLI rows.
- New "Cross-platform portability (v2.0.0)" section documents
  scripts/portability_check.py as the lint authoritative source.
- New per-harness table explains how to load claude-seo on each tool.
- New tool-name compatibility table maps Claude Code tool names
  (Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Glob/Grep/WebFetch) to their equivalents on
  Codex, Cline, Aider, Cursor, and Antigravity. Most harnesses auto-
  alias; the mapping is documented for transparency on recipe-level
  tool calls.

scripts/portability_check.py
- Validates every SKILL.md under skills/ and extensions/ against the
  portable subset that every supported harness accepts:
    * name        — required, kebab-case, < 64 chars
    * description — required, < 1024 chars
    * model, tools, maxTurns, compatibility, metadata — Claude-Code
      friendly fields tolerated by other harnesses
- Severities:
    error    — harness will reject the file
    warning  — file works but loses information on non-Claude harnesses
               (e.g., inline `# comments` in `tools:` parse strangely
               on Cline + Codex)
    info     — noteworthy but harmless
- --strict promotes warnings to errors for CI gates.
- No PyYAML dependency: ships a tiny scalar-string-only parser
  matching the documented frontmatter subset.

Whole-repo smoke: 33 SKILL.md files checked, 0 errors, 0 warnings,
0 info findings.

tests/test_portability.py (10 cases)
- Full-repo lint must pass (regression gate).
- check_one: flags missing frontmatter, missing name, camelCase name,
  missing description, warns on inline comments in tools.
- AGENTS.md must mention every target harness (Cursor, Gemini CLI,
  Codex, Cline, Aider, Antigravity).
- AGENTS.md must document portability_check.py.
- AGENTS.md tool-mapping table must include Codex + Cline + Aider
  columns and Read/Write/Edit/Bash/WebFetch rows.

Phase I reframings + deprecations (already in earlier checkpoints):
- Checkpoint 4 shipped skills/seo-schema/references/
  deprecated-types-2024-2026.md (Vehicle Listing / Claim Review /
  Estimated Salary / Learning Video / Course Info carousel /
  Special Announcement / HowTo / FAQ).
- Checkpoint 5 shipped skills/seo-geo/references/llmstxt-evidence.md
  (Mueller + Illyes + SE Ranking + Otterly evidence reframe).
- Checkpoint 6 shipped skills/seo-google/references/
  dma-consent-mode-v2.md (Google's July 2024 third-party-cookie
  reversal + April 2025 confirmation; cookieless framing softened).
- FID sanity grep: every remaining FID mention is an "INP replaced
  FID" migration note (correct, intentional). No residual usage.

.github/workflows/ci.yml
- Added portability_check.py to py_compile (47 -> 48 scripts).

Test count: 237 -> 247 (+10). Local sync_flow tests intermittently
flake on the GitHub anonymous 60/hr rate limit after repeated runs;
ci.yml uses GH_TOKEN to lift the cap.

Local-only commit on v2. No push.

add given-names, family-names, email, website per cff schema

Prior state had only `alias: AgriciDaniel`. The Citation File Format 1.2
schema treats an `authors` entry as valid with alias alone, but cffconvert
and academic citation tools (Zenodo, Zotero) warn or fall back to "Anonymous"
when given-names / family-names are absent. Adding them lifts the metadata
to full schema compliance.

Fields added (all under the existing single-author entry):
- given-names: Daniel
- family-names: Agrici
- email: agricidaniel@gmail.com  (matches CODEOWNERS contact, public profile)
- website: https://agricidaniel.com

Blast radius: citation tooling only. No code path reads CITATION.cff.
Undo: revert. YAML well-formedness verified." class="Link--secondary" href="/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/commit/43e969aa9c1be6b05a7d2cacaff4477c1d636698">fix(citation): add given-names, family-names, email, website per public origin is touched only on release.

* docs(readme): structural cleanup, drift fixes, em-dash and filler sweep

Slate (R1-R11 from audit) plus the em-dash and filler-word constraints:

- Drop Showcase section (third-party project listing not appropriate
  for marketplace README; can move to SHOWCASE.md or Discussions later)
- Drop Publishing Pipeline section (Rankenstein cross-promo)
- Fold MCP Integrations content into the Extensions intro paragraph
  (was wrongly nested under Uninstall)
- Consolidate two trailing footer blocks into a single Author section
- Drop duplicated AI Marketing Hub link (kept in Community Contributors
  where context is clearer)
- Architecture skill counts updated: 21+3 sub-skills became 24, 15+2
  sub-agents became 18 (matches Phase A canonical phrasing)
- Drop version markers from feature headers ("New in v1.6.0", "v1.7.0",
  "New", "New for 2026") so the README stops rotting per-release
- Drop the Recently Added section (CHANGELOG is the canonical source)
- Tighten the curl details block to one safe variant (review then run)
- Drop the stale Updated comment at the top
- ToC reordered to match document order, plus the previously missing
  entries (Community Contributors, License, Author)

Style:
- Zero em dashes
- Zero -- used as em-dash substitute in prose
- Filler removed: Comprehensive, fully, Enterprise, New for 2026

Verified: ToC anchors resolve, markdown still parses, line count
446 to 389.

- New seo-geo skill for AI Overviews / GEO optimization

Security:
- lxml minimum version 5.3.2 (CVE-2025-24928 fix)
- Bounded dependency pinning

Features:
- 9 commands: audit, page, technical, content, schema, geo, images, sitemap, plan
- 6 industry templates: SaaS, local service, e-commerce, publisher, agency, generic
- Quality gates for location pages (30+ warning, 50+ hard stop)
- Parallel subagent delegation for audits

class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="chore(governance): SSS+ tier repo hygiene — CODEOWNERS, npm Dependabot, v2 workflow, SECURITY.md uplift

Closes the Phase H GitHub-skill governance gaps identified during the
v2 self-audit.

SECURITY.md
-----------
Expanded from 25 to 130 lines covering:
- Reporting channels: GitHub Security Advisory (preferred) + email
  fallback + PGP-on-request workflow.
- Coordinated disclosure: explicit 90-day timeline with per-day
  milestones (ack ≤72h, triage ≤3d, fix candidate ≤14d, release ≤30d,
  public advisory ≤90d).
- Supported versions matrix: 2.x active, 1.9.x patch-only for
  CVSS≥High, <1.9 unsupported.
- Three-axis threat model: malicious audit target, tampered install,
  local credential escalation. Each axis lists its specific
  mitigation by file path.
- Known residual risks listed honestly:
  * Playwright/Chromium TOCTOU (Python-layer pin can't reach the
    renderer; route handler is defence-in-depth).
  * IPv6-only audit targets refused (fail-closed).
  * Windows file permissions (fchmod is a no-op on non-ACL FS).
- High-leverage code-path index for auditors.

.github/CODEOWNERS
------------------
New. Default owner @AgriciDaniel. Security-critical paths called out
explicitly: url_safety, google_auth, backlinks_auth, render_page,
fetch_page, capture_screenshot, test_url_safety, SECURITY.md, and
the release-channel files (install.sh/.ps1, plugin.json,
marketplace.json, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, CITATION.cff).
A future co-maintainer cannot land a change to any of these without
a review request reaching the right reviewer.

.github/dependabot.yml
----------------------
+ npm ecosystem watcher (weekly Monday 06:00 UTC, labels
  dependencies/npm/extensions). v2's MCP extensions (DataForSEO,
  Firecrawl, Ahrefs, etc.) install via `npx`; the npm watcher
  tracks the pinned versions in any package.json we ship.

.github/workflows/v2.yml
------------------------
New, fires on workflow_dispatch + workflow_call only — NEVER on
push or pull_request. Stays dormant until the v2 branch is pushed;
after the push, the maintainer triggers it manually from the
Actions tab to gate the v2.0.0 tag.

Three jobs:
- manifest: pytest tests/test_manifest_consistency.py (13 assertions)
- security: pytest tests/test_url_safety.py + py_compile of every
  security-critical module (url_safety, render_page, fetch_page,
  capture_screenshot, google_auth, backlinks_auth)
- full_pytest: complete tests/ suite
Audit summary step emits all three results to the GitHub Actions
summary so the gating decision is one screen away from the tag.

.github/workflows/ci.yml
------------------------
+ scripts/url_safety.py and scripts/render_page.py to the py_compile
  list (closes the gap where new safety/render code would land
  without syntax-checking on every push to main).
+ Script count comment updated from "30" to "32".

Tests still green: 122 passed.
Local-only commit on v2. No push.

foundation - shared SSRF-safe URL layer + headless renderer

Lays the foundation for v2 Phase A (headless rendering everywhere) and
closes v1.9.0-deferred security items DNS rebinding + backlinks_auth
fallback regression.

New scripts/url_safety.py
- Canonical SSRF + DNS-rebinding mitigation for the whole project.
- validate_url(url) preserves v1.9.9 boolean contract.
- validate_url_strict(url) resolves DNS and refuses any non-public A
  record (pinned IP returned for use by safe_requests_get).
- safe_requests_get / safe_requests_session wrap requests.get inside a
  contextmanager that monkey-patches socket.getaddrinfo so the OS-level
  resolver can only return the pre-validated IP for the duration of
  the call. TLS SNI + HTTP Host preserved; thread-safety guarded by a
  non-blocking lock that raises rather than corrupts global state.
- Extended hostname block list to every documented cloud-metadata
  endpoint (AWS, Azure, GCP, Oracle, Alibaba) including 169.254.169.254
  and fd00:ec2::254 for defence-in-depth and clearer errors.

New scripts/render_page.py
- Shared Playwright Chromium renderer that every audit subagent can
  call. Three modes: auto (raw fetch, render only on SPA detection),
  always, never.
- SPA detector covers React/Next.js/Vue/Nuxt/Svelte/Astro shells plus
  a thin-body heuristic tuned to 100 chars (verified against
  example.com false-positive at 125 chars).
- trafilatura main-content extraction + htmldate publication-date
  extraction post-render; both optional, never block the audit.
- Playwright route() handler aborts any subresource request whose
  hostname resolves to a non-public IP (defence-in-depth against
  rebinding inside Chromium's resolver).

scripts/google_auth.py
- validate_url is now a thin back-compat wrapper around
  url_safety.validate_url. No behaviour change for callers.

scripts/backlinks_auth.py
- Removed the v1.9.0 fallback that omitted IP-range checks (flagged
  in the v2 cybersecurity audit). Now hard-fails if url_safety can't
  be imported; better to refuse to run than to skip SSRF protection.

requirements.txt
- trafilatura>=2.0.0,<3.0.0
- htmldate>=1.9.0,<2.0.0
- courlan>=1.3.0,<2.0.0 (trafilatura helper, explicitly pinned to
  avoid transitive drift)

Smoke tests pass: parse-mode 9/9, is_safe_ip 7/7, end-to-end DNS-pinned
fetch against example.com (200, getaddrinfo restored), private-IP
literal refusal, SPA heuristic across React/Next/thin-body cases.

Local-only commit on the v2 branch. No push.

title="fix(uninstall): use glob enumeration so future skills are cleaned up (#88)

The uninstall scripts had been frozen at v1.4.0-era state and used a
hardcoded list of 12 sub-skills and 7 agents. Between v1.5 and v1.9.8 we
added 12 sub-skills and 11 sub-agents that the uninstaller never knew
about. Anyone running 'bash uninstall.sh' today gets half a cleanup.

Both uninstall.sh (bash) and uninstall.ps1 (pwsh) now enumerate skills
via glob matching:
- skills/seo (orchestrator)
- skills/seo-* (any sub-skill)
- agents/seo-*.md (any agent file)

This auto-tracks future additions without requiring uninstaller
maintenance per release. Sibling skills under ~/.claude/skills/ that do
not start with 'seo' (e.g. blog-writer, security) are explicitly NOT
touched.

Sandbox-tested with 8 assertions covering:
- orchestrator removal
- old hardcoded list (seo-audit) still works
- new skills (seo-content-brief, seo-flow) now cleaned up
- non-seo skills (blog-writer, security) preserved
- agent file removal
- non-seo agent files preserved
- empty-installation edge case (exit 0 with helpful message)

Plugin-install users (the recommended path) use /plugin uninstall and
are unaffected by this change. The bash and ps1 scripts are for the
manual-install path that docs/INSTALLATION.md documents.

title="fix(uninstall): use glob enumeration so future skills are cleaned up (#88)

The uninstall scripts had been frozen at v1.4.0-era state and used a
hardcoded list of 12 sub-skills and 7 agents. Between v1.5 and v1.9.8 we
added 12 sub-skills and 11 sub-agents that the uninstaller never knew
about. Anyone running 'bash uninstall.sh' today gets half a cleanup.

Both uninstall.sh (bash) and uninstall.ps1 (pwsh) now enumerate skills
via glob matching:
- skills/seo (orchestrator)
- skills/seo-* (any sub-skill)
- agents/seo-*.md (any agent file)

This auto-tracks future additions without requiring uninstaller
maintenance per release. Sibling skills under ~/.claude/skills/ that do
not start with 'seo' (e.g. blog-writer, security) are explicitly NOT
touched.

Sandbox-tested with 8 assertions covering:
- orchestrator removal
- old hardcoded list (seo-audit) still works
- new skills (seo-content-brief, seo-flow) now cleaned up
- non-seo skills (blog-writer, security) preserved
- agent file removal
- non-seo agent files preserved
- empty-installation edge case (exit 0 with helpful message)

Plugin-install users (the recommended path) use /plugin uninstall and
are unaffected by this change. The bash and ps1 scripts are for the
manual-install path that docs/INSTALLATION.md documents.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/commit/56cb16ab14ff4cfed15b6b5230dba5f26d7d6f53">fix(uninstall): use glob <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Claude SEO: SEO Analysis Skill for Claude Code</h1><a id="user-content-claude-seo-seo-analysis-skill-for-claude-code" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Claude SEO: SEO Analysis Skill for Claude Code" href="#claude-seo-seo-analysis-skill-for-claude-code"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Claude SEO is an open-source SEO analysis plugin for <a href="https://claude.ai/claude-code" rel="nofollow">Claude Code</a>.</strong> It runs 25 sub-skills and 18 specialist agents in parallel across technical SEO, content quality (E-E-A-T), Schema.org markup, AI search optimization (GEO), local SEO, e-commerce, and international SEO. Every audit produces a prioritized action plan with falsifiable recommendations grounded in primary-source guidance from Google.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/actions/workflows/ci.yml"><img src="https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg" alt="CI" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Two versions of this skill.</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>🌐 <strong>Public open-source</strong> → <a href="https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo"><code>AgriciDaniel/claude-seo</code></a>: MIT, public releases, no membership. Use this if you want stable + downloadable.</li>
<li>🔒 <strong>Community private mirror</strong> → <a href="https://github.com/AI-Marketing-Hub/claude-seo"><code>AI-Marketing-Hub/claude-seo</code></a>: early access to upcoming features and direct collaboration with the <a href="https://www.skool.com/ai-marketing-hub-pro" rel="nofollow">AI Marketing Hub Pro</a> community. Requires membership.</li>
</ul>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why Claude SEO</h3><a id="user-content-why-claude-seo" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why Claude SEO" href="#why-claude-seo"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>AI-search first.</strong> Aligned with <a href="https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide" rel="nofollow">Google's AI Optimization Guide</a>. Question-based citability scoring, primary-source evidence on llms.txt, IPTC <code>TrainedAlgorithmicMedia</code> for AI-generated product images, agent-friendly page checks per <a href="https://web.dev/" rel="nofollow">web.dev</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Parallel execution.</strong> Full site audits spawn up to 15 specialist agents simultaneously. Site-level audits complete in minutes rather than hours.</li>
<li><strong>Falsifiable, not promotional.</strong> Every recommendation carries the first-principle observation it rests on, its dependency relationships, an explicit "how would we know this failed?" check, and a leading indicator. See <a href="#methodology">Methodology</a>.</li>
</ul>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">Using Codex instead of Claude Code? Use <a href="https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/codex-seo">Codex SEO</a>, the Codex-first port with TOML agents, plugin packaging, deterministic runners, and the same SEO workflow surface.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Who this is for</h2><a id="user-content-who-this-is-for" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Who this is for" href="#who-this-is-for"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>SEO agencies running 5+ client sites.</strong> Replace quarterly deep audits with weekly automated runs. Same team capacity, 4× audit cadence, every recommendation comes with a falsifiability check the client can verify.</li>
<li><strong>In-house SEO leads at SaaS / publisher / e-commerce companies.</strong> Second-pair-of-eyes before executive reviews. Catches what GSC and Lighthouse hide: schema deprecation, AI-citability gaps, expired-domain heritage risk, parasite-SEO exposure, machine-translation drift.</li>
<li><strong>Freelance SEO consultants.</strong> Anchor day-one client scope with a 15-minute audit and a real 0-100 score. Win the engagement with concrete proof of value before you spend an hour writing the proposal.</li>
</ul>

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">ℹ️ <strong>Which version are you installing?</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Public open-source (default).</strong> The commands below install from <a href="https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo"><code>AgriciDaniel/claude-seo</code></a> — MIT, public releases, no membership required.</li>
<li><strong>AI Marketing Hub Pro member?</strong> Install the community version with early access instead: swap <code>AgriciDaniel/claude-seo</code> for <code>AI-Marketing-Hub/claude-seo</code> and the plugin slug <code>claude-seo@agricidaniel-claude-seo</code> for <code>claude-seo@ai-marketing-hub-claude-seo</code>. Requires <code>gh auth login</code> (or PAT) with access to the <code>AI-Marketing-Hub</code> org. If <code>/plugin marketplace add</code> 404s, DM in the <a href="https://www.skool.com/ai-marketing-hub-pro" rel="nofollow">Skool community</a> to get added.</li>
</ul>
</blockquote>

<p dir="auto"><strong>Why <code>git clone</code> instead of <code>irm | iex</code>?</strong> Claude Code's own security guardrails flag <code>irm ... | iex</code> as a supply chain risk: downloading and executing remote code without verification. <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Features</h2><a id="user-content-features" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Features" href="#features"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Core Web Vitals does Claude SEO check?</h3><a id="user-content-what-core-web-vitals-does-claude-seo-check" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Core Web Vitals does Claude SEO check?" href="#what-core-web-vitals-does-claude-seo-check"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Claude SEO measures the current three Core Web Vitals: <strong>LCP</strong> (Largest Contentful Paint, target under 2.5s), <strong>INP</strong> (Interaction to Next Paint, target under 200ms), and <strong>CLS</strong> (Cumulative Layout Shift, target under 0.1). <a href="https://web.dev/articles/inp" rel="nofollow">INP replaced FID</a> on March 12, 2024; FID was removed from all Chrome tools (CrUX API, PageSpeed Insights, Lighthouse) on September 9, 2024, and Claude SEO never references FID. Field data comes from the Chrome User Experience Report (CrUX) when available; lab data falls back to Lighthouse via PageSpeed Insights. LCP can be decomposed into subparts (TTFB, load delay, load duration, render delay) via the <code>/seo google</code> CrUX integration to localize bottlenecks. Mobile and desktop are measured separately. CrUX History (25-week trend) is included in the Tier 0 free credential set.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How does Claude SEO assess E-E-A-T?</h3><a id="user-content-how-does-claude-seo-assess-e-e-a-t" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How does Claude SEO assess E-E-A-T?" href="#how-does-claude-seo-assess-e-e-a-t"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) is evaluated against the Search Quality Rater Guidelines, last updated September 2025 with YMYL expanded to include political and social topics. Experience signals: original research, case studies, first-hand photos. Expertise: author credentials and topical depth. Authoritativeness: external citations and brand mentions. Trustworthiness, the most heavily weighted of the four: contact info, secure HTTPS, transparent corrections, date stamps. Before scoring sub-factors, Claude SEO applies Google's own Who / How / Why heuristic from the <a href="https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/creating-helpful-content" rel="nofollow">helpful-content guide</a>. Generative AI content is fine if it meets Search Essentials; it crosses into spam when used to scale low-value pages, which <code>seo-content humanize</code> and <code>seo-content verify</code> are designed to detect.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Schema.org types does Claude SEO support?</h3><a id="user-content-what-schemaorg-types-does-claude-seo-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Schema.org types does Claude SEO support?" href="#what-schemaorg-types-does-claude-seo-support"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">JSON-LD is the preferred format (Google's stated preference). Active types Claude SEO detects, validates, and generates: Organization, LocalBusiness, Article, BlogPosting, NewsArticle, Product, ProductGroup, Offer, Review, AggregateRating, BreadcrumbList, WebSite, WebPage, Person, ProfilePage, ContactPage, VideoObject, ImageObject, Event, JobPosting, Course, DiscussionForumPosting, Reservation, OrderAction, plus video and specialized types (BroadcastEvent, Clip, SeekToAction, SoftwareSourceCode). Restricted: FAQ (government and healthcare authority sites only since August 2023). Deprecated and never recommended: HowTo (rich results removed September 2023), SpecialAnnouncement (July 2025), ClaimReview, VehicleListing, EstimatedSalary, LearningVideo, CourseInfo carousel (all retired June 2025). Replacement guidance: <a href="/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/skills/seo-schema/references/deprecated-types-2024-2026.md">skills/seo-schema/references/deprecated-types-2024-2026.md</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How does Claude SEO optimize for AI search?</h3><a id="user-content-how-does-claude-seo-optimize-for-ai-search" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How does Claude SEO optimize for AI search?" href="#how-does-claude-seo-optimize-for-ai-search"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Aligned with <a href="https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/ai-optimization-guide" rel="nofollow">Google's AI Optimization Guide</a>, which states that "AEO" and "GEO" are rebranded labels for SEO. AI Overviews and AI Mode are grounded in the same ranking systems as classic Search; pages must be indexed and eligible for snippet display to appear in any AI feature. Claude SEO scores passage citability (optimal 134-167 word self-contained answer blocks), question-based heading hierarchy, attribution density, structured data coverage, and entity presence across Wikipedia, Reddit, YouTube, and LinkedIn. The <code>seo-geo</code> skill includes evidence-based reframes of three popular myths: llms.txt is not currently a citation lever (<a href="/AgriciDaniel/claude-seo/blob/main/skills/seo-geo/references/llmstxt-evidence.md">primary-source evidence</a>), content chunking is not required, and AI-specific keyword rewriting is unnecessary because synonym understanding is sufficient.</p>

<p dir="auto">A 4-tier credential system lets you start with zero keys and add data as needed. Every tier delivers real value at its level:</p>
